---
title: "Patient-robust discovery of combinatorial cell-surface targets in prostate cancer"
subtitle: "AND-gate and NOT-gate marker pairs from single-cell data, benchmarked against PSMA-PSCA"
author: "William Guesdon"
date: now
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm"
abstract: |
  Single-antigen therapies against solid tumors are limited by on-target off-tumor toxicity, because
  few surface antigens are truly absent from all healthy tissue. Combinatorial targeting requires two
  conditions before a cell is engaged, which can recover specificity. We search public single-cell
  data for pairs of cell-surface proteins that discriminate prostate cancer cells from every healthy
  human cell type, under two logics: AND (co-expression of both markers on the tumor) and NOT
  (an activator on the tumor with a blocker that spares healthy cells expressing the activator). Pairs
  are scored per patient and summarized across patients, so no single high-cell-count patient drives a
  result. The known PSMA-PSCA pair serves as a positive control the method should recover. Protein-level
  evidence from the Human Protein Atlas and PaxDb qualifies surface localization and normal-tissue risk.
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    css: styles/report.css
    title-block-banner: false
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
bibliography: references.bib
---

<!--
This is the report skeleton. It mirrors the structure used in the SatMut project: every number in
the prose is bound to a committed table in results/tables/, and the report renders from those tables
with no model or network call. Fill each section as the pipeline produces its table. Do not paste
numbers by hand; read them from the CSVs so the report and the evidence never drift.
-->

## Background

<!-- The therapeutic problem: on-target off-tumor toxicity, and why combinatorial logic gating helps.
     Cite the clinical context (PSMA-PSCA AND gate, STEAP1 engager, A2 Bio Tmod NOT gate). -->

## Data

<!-- Tumor: HuPSA and an independent replication cohort. Healthy: Tabula Sapiens via CELLxGENE Census.
     Protein: Human Protein Atlas, PaxDb. State cell counts, patient counts, and cell-type labels. -->

## Methods

<!-- Surface-gene panel. Per-patient AND and NOT scoring. Pareto ranking on the lower patient quantile
     of tumor coverage against the worst-case healthy-cell-type liability. Thresholding sensitivity. -->

## Results

### Positive control: recovery of PSMA-PSCA

<!-- Show the benchmark pair recovered from the unsupervised scan. -->

### Ranked AND-gate pairs

### Exploratory NOT-gate pairs

<!-- Flag the dropout caveat: NOT-gate calls depend on trusting B-negatives. -->

## Limitations

<!-- Episomal vs endogenous, scRNA dropout, transcript is not targetability, bulk proteomics cannot
     prove same-cell co-expression. The validation experiments that would come next. -->

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
